scenario: April 6 8pm ET deadline active; US F-15E downed over Iran (both crew rescued); Kuwait desalination offline; AIS transits -95% from historical avg

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Fertilizer cost pass-through: Gulf urea still +90% since Hormuz closure; Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation reverses Apr 1 relief. Iran UAE attack (Apr 2) reinforces no supply chain resolution; Egypt panic-buying continues. April 6 deadline raises acute planting-season supply risk for Asia rice belts. Source: Barchart ZWK26.

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel demand elevated as jet fuel rebounds on Brent +8.3%. Indonesian ethanol mandate acceleration competes with feed use. Trump 'Stone Ages' reversal + UAE attack removes Apr 1 relief; fertilizer cost uplift on next planting cycle persists; Tehran Toll Booth active.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker cost spike resumes on Brent +8.3% (Trump 'Stone Ages' reversal). Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins under continued pressure from elevated freight. Limited direct Hormuz exposure - US/South America origin - caps upside vs energy commodities but freight overhang persists.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: Crude oil correlation rebounding on Brent +8.3% (Trump 'Stone Ages'). Biodiesel feedstock demand elevated; Indonesia B40 mandate intact. Fertilizer disruption threatening next Indonesian yield cycle. Iran UAE attack Apr 2 removes any near-term resolution signal. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security alarm: fertilizer shortfall threatens Vietnam and Thailand rice belts. Philippines, Indonesia importing at distressed freight premiums - Philippines bilateral Hormuz deal helps shipping but not fertilizer access. India export ban in place. Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation eliminates near-term resolution path.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Gulf condensate-derived naphtha rebounding on Brent +8.3% (Trump 'Stone Ages'). Tehran Toll Booth active; Qatar force majeure unresolved; Iran UAE attack (457 missiles) removes any near-term supply restoration path. Jurong Island and Map Ta Phut crackers remain at reduced rates. Source: SGX NJF1 futures.

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Downstream from naphtha surge. SABIC and QAPCO ethylene exports still bottlenecked; naphtha escalation passes through with a lag. SE Asian PE and PVC producers see marginal cost reversal; agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times still 6-8 weeks. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia.

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Iran is world's 2nd largest methanol exporter - production disrupted directly by conflict. Qatar force majeure in effect. China MTO complex facing feedstock squeeze. Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation removes any near-term Iran production restart; UAE attack adds further uncertainty. Partial offset from US and Chinese domestic supply. Source: Investing.com CFR China futures.

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Qafco (Qatar) force majeure in effect since Mar 2. Saudi SAFCO and SABIC urea plants at ~60% capacity. Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation + UAE attack (457 missiles) eliminates production restart path. Iranian parliament legislating Tehran Toll Booth - Hormuz access structurally uncertain. Egypt and India panic-buying from Russia/China at large premiums; April 6 deadline raises acute risk of further Saudi capacity cuts. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers.

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: Saudi Ma'aden DAP shipping disrupted; Toll Booth transit cost adds $20-30/mt delivered premium. Iran UAE attack raises Saudi output-cut risk, adding supply-side uncertainty. US Gulf filling gap at premium freight. Philippines and Vietnam facing import cost spikes ahead of wet-season planting.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Less directly Hormuz-exposed (Canada, Russia main suppliers) but elevated freight costs add indirect pressure. Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation removes war-premium relief. Palm oil plantations (Indonesia, Malaysia) are major users - any yield disruption from fertilizer shortage amplifies CPO supply concern.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: Cape re-routing absorbing dry bulk vessel capacity as iron ore, coal, and grain shipments divert around southern Africa. Australia-Japan coal routing Cape adding 12 days. Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation eliminates any near-term Hormuz routing recovery - vessels committed to Cape through at least Q2. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News.

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: Tehran Toll Booth active ($2M/vessel in yuan); VLCC owners face dual pricing structure. Trump 'Stone Ages' escalation + Iran UAE attack (457 missiles, 11 dead) fully reverses Apr 1 war-risk relief. P&I restoration impossible until political settlement. ~35% of VLCC fleet war-risk excluded; April 6 Kharg seizure risk premium surging. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List.

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Asia-Europe container rates rebounding as Trump 'Stone Ages' signal eliminates near-term Suez Canal restoration. UAE attack disrupts Dubai port operations; Kuwait Airport fuel damage ongoing. Philippines bilateral Hormuz deal offers marginal relief for Manila-Gulf routes only. SE Asian manufacturers still facing extended lead times. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange.
